stem: In the electron transport chain, what is the direct role of oxygen?

choices.A: It is the final electron acceptor.
choices.C: It phosphorylates ADP into ATP directly.
choices.D: It catalyzes the reactions of the citric acid cycle.

B.misconception: Reverses the electron donor/acceptor roles of O2
B.rationale: Choosing B means the student does not hold the fact that O2 is the terminal acceptor; the donor/acceptor concept is inverted, a declarative gap.

C.misconception: Conflates O2's role with ATP synthase's phosphorylation step
C.rationale: The student knows phosphorylation happens at the end of respiration but mis-attributes the mechanism to O2 - an integration/application error, not a missing fact.

D.misconception: Associates O2 with an unrelated pathway stage (citric acid cycle)
D.rationale: Selecting a citric-acid-cycle role reflects an absent link between O2 and the ETC specifically.

authoring.notes: TEMPLATE / illustrative only. v1 taxonomy (content_gap, reasoning_error, misread_or_passage_mapping, abstain) - no careless_slip (needs timing, see spec 3.2). source=real_eval: a real held-out item, gold for scoring, never trained on.

stem: A plant cell is placed in a hypertonic solution. What is the net movement of water?

choices.A: Water leaves the cell, and the cell plasmolyzes.
choices.B: Water enters the cell, and the cell becomes turgid.
choices.C: There is no net movement of water.
choices.D: Solute enters the cell to balance concentrations.

B.misconception: Reverses the direction of osmosis relative to tonicity
B.rationale: Believing water enters in a hypertonic solution is a wrong fact about which way water moves - a declarative gap on the definition of hypertonic.

C.misconception: Assumes equilibrium is already reached instead of reasoning about the gradient
C.rationale: The student knows water moves down its gradient but fails to apply that a concentration difference exists, so they under-reason rather than mis-recall.

D.misconception: Confuses osmosis (water movement) with solute diffusion across the membrane
D.rationale: Expecting solute to freely cross reflects a missing fact about selective permeability.

authoring.notes: TEMPLATE / illustrative only. v1 taxonomy - no careless_slip. source=real_seed: a real item used to SEED synthetic training variants (its real distractors carry pedagogical validity into synthetic data, see spec 9.2).

passage: In pea plants, seed color (yellow Y is dominant to green y) and seed shape (round R is dominant to wrinkled r) assort independently. A true-breeding yellow, round plant (YYRR) is crossed with a true-breeding green, wrinkled plant (yyrr). The resulting F1 plants are then self-crossed to produce the F2 generation.
stem: In the F2 generation, what fraction of offspring are expected to be green AND round?

B.misconception: Applies the 9/16 double-dominant ratio to the wrong phenotype combination
B.rationale: The student knows the 9:3:3:1 ratio but mis-maps 9/16 (both dominant) onto green+round (recessive+dominant) - a procedural mis-application of a known rule.

C.misconception: Uses the 1/16 double-recessive ratio for a single-recessive phenotype
C.rationale: Selecting 1/16 mis-applies the both-recessive fraction; the machinery is known but the combination is computed wrong.

D.misconception: Answers for a single trait (monohybrid 1/4) instead of the two-trait question
D.rationale: 1/4 is the green fraction alone; the student mapped the stem to one trait and missed that the question asks for the joint (green AND round) outcome - a passage-to-question mapping failure.

authoring.notes: TEMPLATE / illustrative only. v1 taxonomy - no careless_slip. Passage/experimental-design stem. source=real_eval: real held-out item, gold for scoring.

passage: A researcher places identical dialysis bags, each containing a 20% sucrose solution, into four beakers with different external sucrose concentrations (0%, 10%, 20%, 30%). After 30 minutes, each bag is weighed. The bag in the 0% beaker gained the most mass; the bag in the 30% beaker lost mass; the 20% bag was unchanged.
stem: Which conclusion is best supported by the experimental results?

choices.A: Water moved into bags placed in solutions more dilute than their contents.
choices.B: Sucrose diffused out of every bag until concentrations equalized.
choices.C: The dialysis membrane is permeable to sucrose but not to water.
choices.D: Mass change was caused by random measurement error.

B.misconception: Attributes mass change to solute movement instead of water movement
B.rationale: The student can read the data but infers the wrong mechanism (sucrose diffusion) - a reasoning/interpretation error on an experimental-design item.

C.misconception: Reverses which molecule the membrane is permeable to
C.rationale: Stating the membrane blocks water but passes sucrose contradicts the basic fact of selective permeability in this setup - a declarative gap.

D.misconception: Ignores the systematic gradient-dependent pattern described in the passage
D.rationale: Calling a clear, ordered trend 'random error' means the student did not map the passage's described pattern to the question - a comprehension/mapping failure.

authoring.notes: TEMPLATE / illustrative only. v1 taxonomy - no careless_slip. Experimental-design passage stem (MCAT Skill 4). source=real_eval: real held-out item, gold for scoring.

stem: Under anaerobic conditions in human muscle cells, why must fermentation regenerate NAD+?

choices.A: To allow glycolysis to continue producing ATP.
choices.B: To directly synthesize additional ATP during fermentation.
choices.C: To provide electrons to the electron transport chain.
choices.D: To break down lactate back into pyruvate.

B.misconception: Believes fermentation itself yields ATP rather than sustaining glycolysis
B.rationale: The student knows ATP is involved but mis-applies where it comes from; the role of NAD+ regeneration is reasoned incorrectly - a procedural error.

C.misconception: Thinks the ETC operates under anaerobic conditions
C.rationale: Invoking the ETC when no O2 is present reflects a missing fact that the ETC is inactive anaerobically - a declarative gap.

D.misconception: Reverses the direction of the lactate/pyruvate conversion in fermentation
D.rationale: Believing lactate is broken back into pyruvate to regenerate NAD+ mis-states the fermentation reaction - a declarative gap.

authoring.notes: TEMPLATE / illustrative only. v1 taxonomy - no careless_slip. source=synthetic_seeded: TRAINING-only item generated by seeding on real item apbio_cellresp_0001's distractors; never used to report accuracy (only real_eval rows are gold).
